assertions.expectation: Explains that Extended channel provides up to 24 months of support vs 14 months for standard channels
assertions.reason: Response clearly states '24-month support window (vs 14 months on Regular/Stable)' in the benefits section

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended channel has additional cost (it's not free)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions 'Additional cost during extended support period (months 15-24 only)' and has a dedicated cost section explaining billing implications

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended channel gets the same versions as Regular but keeps them available longer
assertions.reason: While the response mentions checking version availability and upgrade timing, it doesn't explicitly state that Extended gets the same versions as Regular but keeps them available longer

assertions.expectation: Warns that staying on older versions longer means missing new features and accumulating technical debt
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Potential security/feature lag if you defer minor upgrades too long' as a tradeoff

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel to Extended
assertions.reason: Response provides the exact command: 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --zone ZONE --release-channel extended'

assertions.expectation: Advises checking that the current version (1.31) is available on Extended before switching
assertions.reason: Response includes Step 1 with specific command to check if 1.31 is available in Extended channel and warns about being 'ahead of channel'

assertions.expectation: Recommends Extended for environments with compliance requirements or slow internal change processes
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Better for compliance environments' and 'ideal for regulated environments (financial services, healthcare)' where predictable upgrade cycles are valued

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended doesn't mean you can skip upgrades forever — you still must upgrade within 24 months
assertions.reason: Response explains the 24-month lifecycle and states 'Cost continues until you upgrade to a version in standard support' implying eventual upgrade requirement

assertions.expectation: Notes that minor version upgrades on Extended are NOT automated (except at EoS) — customers must plan and initiate them manually
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Manual control over minor version upgrades' and 'Minor upgrades become manual' with detailed manual upgrade process section
